brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer B=0 with valid cycle-by-cycle derivation; goes beyond surface by identifying the balancing loop, fixed point, and why D never activates; shows careful attention to update ordering but doesn't reflect on potential reasoning errors; shows all numerical steps clearly but lacks counterfactual or boundary verification; states conclusions clearly but doesn't address what assumptions would change the result; only follows one path without considering e.g. what if A had exceeded T or if no floor existed; every step is fully derivable by a third party from the response alone; explicitly identifies feedback loop and avoids treating the system as one-way cause-effect; avoids both suppressed failure modes and addresses them explicitly in the conclusion; shows structured loop-aware reasoning but does not walk the topology's explicit gate sequence (G1→G2→G3→G4)
